articles.title: 'China stole data of 220m US voters': Trump blames Beijing as he releases files on 2020 polls
articles.description: President Donald Trump alleged China compromised 220 million American voter files. He announced declassification of intelligence documents regarding election interference. Trump claimed intelligence officials concealed this information from him and Congress. He stated that election systems are vulnerable and easily compromised. This announcement comes as Republicans prepare for upcoming midterm elections.

articles.title: 'Hasn't scored in two games': India coach's verdict on Rohit Sharma amid poor form
articles.description: Rohit's struggles were visible in the second ODI in Cardiff on Thursday, where England won by four wickets to level the three-match series 1-1. The opener never looked settled, scoring just 10 runs off 18 balls by the end of the Powerplay even as India raced to 61/1. He eventually made 26 off 47 balls before being dismissed while attempting a sweep against Will Jacks.

articles.title: Mark Cuban's 'warning' to Americans: AI risks making US healthcare worse
articles.description: Mark Cuban cautions that artificial intelligence alone will not solve healthcare issues. He believes insurers will use AI to hinder doctor and patient treatment approvals. Cuban notes that conglomerates already employ AI to manipulate contracts and delay payments. He states that companies lack knowledge of their actual healthcare purchase costs. Cuban advocates for disintermediation to eliminate unnecessary middlemen in healthcare.

articles.title: FBI arrests gangster Nitish Kaushal days after adding him to most wanted list
articles.description: The FBI arrested Indian-origin Nitish Kaushal in Vermont as part of a crackdown. Kaushal is wanted for alleged involvement in murder and drug trafficking. He carried out violent acts for the Bhagwanpuria organised crime group. This arrest follows Operation Hard Ball targeting the Bhagwanpuria gang internationally. Federal prosecutors also charged Lawrence Bishnoi and Goldy Brar with ordering an assassination.

articles.title: Trump's teleprompter operator under federal probe over alleged $90,000 Kalshi insider trading bets
articles.description: Donald Trump's teleprompter operator, Gabriel Perez, faces federal investigation for alleged insider trading. He is currently on unpaid leave from the White House after this development. The investigation focuses on trades made on prediction market platform Kalshi. Kalshi detected suspicious activity and alerted regulators, freezing Perez's account. Perez is reportedly cooperating with the ongoing federal inquiry into the matter.

articles.description: Clavicular has responded after Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez shared concerns about looksmaxxing during an interview with TMZ. AOC said she worries about the pressures young men may face regarding appearance, while Clavicular argued that looksmaxxing is about improving multiple areas of life, including personality and charisma. Their comments have added to the wider debate around self-image and online influence.

articles.title: Indian stabbed 15 times in a mall in US over his religion: Reports
articles.description: An Indian man was allegedly stabbed fifteen times inside a Utah shopping mall. The suspect reportedly asked the victim his religion before the violent assault occurred. Authorities arrested the suspect on suspicion of attempted murder and carrying a dangerous weapon. The victim underwent surgery after being transported to a local hospital for treatment. The Indian consulate is in close contact with the victim's family and local authorities.

articles.title: India's first hydrogen-powered train to be flagged off on July 17: 10 facts and photos you shouldn't miss
articles.description: India’s first hydrogen-powered train is ready for launch! Marking a major milestone for Indian Railways, the country’s first hydrogen will be flagged off by Prime Minister Narendra Modi on July 17. This is the first time that India is introducing a train that produces its own electricity onboard using hydrogen, which is widely regarded as the cleanest fuel available. This also places India among the few countries in the world that are testing hydrogen trains. Why is India’s first hydrogen train project important? What route will the train run on and at what speeds? We take a look at 10 important facts about India’s first hydrogen train that you shouldn’t miss:
articles.full_text: India’s first hydrogen-powered train is ready for launch! Marking a major milestone for Indian Railways, the country’s first hydrogen will be flagged off by Prime Minister Narendra Modi on July 17. This is the first time that India is introducing a train that produces its own electricity onboard using hydrogen, which is widely regarded as the cleanest fuel available. This also places India among the few countries in the world that are testing hydrogen trains. Why is India’s first hydrogen train project important? What route will the train run on and at what speeds? We take a look at 10 important facts about India’s first hydrogen train that you shouldn’t miss: Indian Railways’ first hydrogen train will run between Jind and Sonipat in Haryana. On the 89 kilometres route the train will stop at Pandu Pindara Junction, Lalit Khera Halt, Bhambhewa, Isapur Kheri Halt, Butane Halt, Khandrai Halt, Rabrah Halt, Lath Halt, Mohana, Barwasni Halt. India’s first hydrogen first powered train is designed for speeds of up to 110 kilometres per hour. The train will run at a maximum operational speed of 75 kilometres per hour. The Hydrogen train generates power by combining hydrogen with oxygen to produce electricity. The resultant by-product is only water vapour. It is a near-zero emissions operation, making it one of the cleanest fuel options available. This marks a milestone for Indian Railways in green mobility. The hydrogen-powered train has two hydrogen driving power cars and eight trailer coaches. Each driving power car has hydrogen fuel cells, lithium iron phosphate batteries and hydrogen storage cylinders, which operate together to generate traction power. Positioned at either end of the train, the two driving power cars each generate 1,200 kW (1,600 hp), providing a combined output sufficient to propel the entire train at speeds of up to 110 kmph. Where will the hydrogen train be refueled? Like petrol and CNG-powered vehicles require dedicated filling stations, the hydrogen train also needs specialised refuelling infrastructure. To support its operations, Indian Railways has set up the country's largest hydrogen refuelling facility for the railway network at Jind in Haryana. The hydrogen-fueled train has been designed, engineered and integrated in India using indigenous technology, highlighting the country's expanding capabilities in advanced railway engineering. RDSO prepared the technical specifications and led the design approval process, while Medha Servo Drives integrated the trainset. The Integral Coach Factory was responsible for the train's exterior. To ensure safe operations, a comprehensive, multi-layered safety architecture has been designed to detect hydrogen leaks, excessive heat, flames and smoke. This corridor has been chosen to demonstrate the safety, reliability and operational feasibility of hydrogen-powered passenger services under normal operating conditions. World over, most hydrogen-powered passenger trains that are currently in service have 2-3 coaches - mainly used on short regional corridors. Indian Railways has rolled out a 10-coach passenger train. It is capable of carrying around 2,600 passengers. Hydrogen-powered passenger trains remain at an early stage of development across the world, making India among the few countries that are venturing into this technology. Germany was the first country to introduce commercial hydrogen train services, while France, Italy, China, Japan and several other nations are either testing the technology through pilot projects or deploying it on a limited scale. Indian Railways is evaluating the use of hydrogen-powered trains on heritage railway routes. This includes the Kalka-Shimla section, drawing on the operational experience gained from the Jind-Sonipat hydrogen train project.

articles.title: Where are gold and silver prices heading? Inflation, oil and Fed bets hold the key
articles.full_text: MCX silver is showing signs of a potential recovery after the recent decline, with prices expected to rebound towards the Rs 227,000 mark in the near term. The metal has a key support level around Rs 218,000, which is likely to limit further losses. As long as prices hold above this level, the short-term technical outlook remains positive. Technical indicators also point to improving prospects. Like gold, most momentum oscillators for MCX silver have entered the oversold zone, suggesting the recent sell-off may have been excessive. Such conditions typically indicate that selling pressure is easing and could pave the way for fresh buying interest and short-covering. If this trend continues, silver prices may witness a gradual recovery over the coming sessions. The weakness in precious metals extended well beyond gold, with silver and platinum group metals posting even steeper losses. Spot silver dropped 3.6% to $55.68 per ounce, while platinum fell 3.1% and palladium declined 4.1%. The broad-based sell-off reflected growing concerns that higher interest rates and a stronger dollar could weigh on demand across the precious metals complex. Investors reduced exposure not only to gold but also to other metals as market sentiment shifted towards interest-bearing assets. The move highlighted how macroeconomic developments can influence the entire precious metals basket rather than affecting gold alone. The rise in US Treasury yields added another layer of pressure on gold prices. Benchmark 10-year Treasury yields moved higher as investors reassessed expectations for the Federal Reserve's policy path. Higher bond yields increase the returns available on fixed-income investments, making non-yielding assets such as gold comparatively less attractive. Analysts noted that the combination of stronger oil prices and expectations of persistent inflation contributed to the move higher in yields. As a result, investors shifted away from bullion despite ongoing geopolitical uncertainties. The reaction underscored the close relationship between the bond market and precious metals, particularly during periods when inflation and interest rate expectations dominate trading sentiment. Escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East emerged as a major factor influencing bullion prices after concerns grew over potential disruptions to global energy supplies. Oil prices remained near a one-month high following reports that Iran had asked Yemen's Houthis to prepare for the possible closure of the Red Sea oil route if US strikes targeted Iranian power infrastructure. Higher crude prices typically raise inflation concerns, prompting investors to expect tighter monetary policy, reducing appeal for the yellow metal, which does not generate interest income. Instead of benefiting from safe-haven buying, bullion came under pressure as the market focused on the inflationary impact of higher energy prices. Gold prices fell sharply after renewed concerns over inflation weakened investor appetite for the precious metal. Spot gold dropped nearly 2% to around $3,984 an ounce, its lowest level in more than two weeks, while US gold futures also settled lower. The decline came as rising crude oil prices fuelled fears that inflation could remain elevated, reducing hopes of interest rate cuts. Since gold does not offer interest income, expectations of higher borrowing costs tend to make the metal less attractive compared to interest-bearing assets. The move also pushed bullion below the closely watched $4,000-an-ounce mark, highlighting how quickly global macroeconomic developments can influence sentiment in precious metals markets.

articles.title: Crude heads for biggest weekly gain in months amid intensifying Middle East crisis
articles.description: Oil prices surged as escalating US-Iran military tensions raised supply disruption concerns. Brent crude traded at $85.34 and WTI crude at $80.08 per barrel. Recent US strikes aimed to degrade Iranian military capabilities, while Iran retaliated against regional allies. Renewed hostilities revived fears of a broader conflict and threats to the Strait of Hormuz. Iran warned of widespread infrastructure damage if US threats to strike its facilities are carried out.

articles.title: Fuel-saving tech may be rewarded
articles.description: New fuel efficiency rules may reward various fuel-saving technologies. Manufacturers can earn compliance benefits for technologies like start-stop systems. These approved technologies will offer credits equivalent to grams of CO2 per kilometer. This framework encourages wider deployment of features across mass-market vehicles. The draft also strengthens the case for flex-fuel vehicles with renewable fuel incentives.

The draft also strengthens the case for flex-fuel vehicles with renewable fuel incentives.

articles.title: ODI tickets worth Rs 52.36 lakh went unsold: Khanna
articles.description: "Tickets worth Rupees 52,36,000 were unsold. Why all the tickets were not sold-out? DDCA started online ticket sale quite late that too without proper advertisement," Khanna claimed in a media release.

articles.title: NEET UG 2026 result declared: AIR 1 Aryan Gupta says ‘Hard work never goes unnoticed’, shares advice for aspirants
articles.description: The NTA has announced the NEET UG 2026 results, with over 11.21 lakh candidates qualifying for medical admissions. Punjab's Aryan Gupta, who secured a joint AIR 1 with 715 marks, credited his success to 16–17 hours of daily study and perseverance. Aspiring to become an oncologist, he urged future aspirants to keep working hard and never lose hope.

articles.title: ISC Class 12 improvement result 2026 declared at cisce.org, rechecking window open till July 20: Check details here
articles.description: The Council for the Indian School Certificate Examinations (CISCE) has released the ISC Class 12 Improvement Result 2026 on its official website, cisce.org. Students can download their revised scorecards using their login credentials. The council has also opened the rechecking window until July 20, while schools have been instructed to complete the process for issuing updated marksheets and certificates.

articles.title: Re-NEET UG 2026 result declared at neet.nta.nic.in; Aryan Gupta, Panshul Bansal top with 715 marks
articles.description: The National Testing Agency has announced the NEET UG 2026 examination results today. Over 11.21 lakh candidates successfully qualified for undergraduate medical and dental courses. Aryan Gupta and Panshul Bansal jointly secured the highest score of 715 marks. More than 58 percent of the qualified candidates are women, with top performers aged 17 to 19. Counselling for medical admissions will commence shortly after this result declaration.

articles.title: Shaji Kailas opens up on 'Red Chillies' failure
articles.description: Filmmaker Shaji Kailas discussed the 2009 film Red Chillies' box office disappointment. He explained that the original script underwent significant changes during production. Mohanlal's role was expanded unexpectedly after his schedule became available. This led to hurried writing which altered the story's foundation. The director believes these last-minute alterations impacted the film's overall connection with audiences.
